metadata.title: Saša Pajić  Product Designer & Engineer
metadata.description: Product designer and engineer crafting systems, interfaces, and experiences that balance clarity with capability.

content.headline: I design systems that make complex things feel simple.
content.subheadline: I'm a product designer who also builds what I design. I focus on information architecture, interaction patterns, and design systems  then bring them to life with code. The result is work where the design decisions and the implementation inform each other.

content.bio:
Most of my work starts with something that doesn't quite make sense yet. A product that works but feels heavier than it should, an interface where users hesitate instead of act, or a system that's grown in directions no one planned for. I like sitting with that ambiguity and figuring out what's actually going on underneath it.

I approach design through the lens of systems thinking. Every interface is part of a larger whole  the data it depends on, the decisions it enables, the workflows it interrupts or supports. I try to understand those relationships before I touch a pixel.

What keeps me interested is work where the outcome has weight. Not just shipping for the sake of shipping, but making something that holds together over time and doesn't treat the person using it like an afterthought. I care about clarity, consistency, and the small choices that shape how a product feels months after launch.

I design with something less rational than logic. When a layout clicks, it's not because I reasoned my way there  it's because something uncomfortable went quiet. I've learned to trust that feeling more than most rules.

I'm based in Valencia, Spain. Open to remote and B2B work.

content.title: Selected Work
content.subtitle: Product design and engineering  from problem framing to production.

items.title: Pragmatic Labs AI
items.subtitle: Marketing Website  Design System & Development
items.description: Designed and built the marketing website for an AI consulting firm  from visual identity through design token system, custom 3D canvas animation, and dark mode implementation.
items.problem: AI consulting firms default to cold, technical aesthetics. The site needed to signal expertise while feeling approachable.
items.insight: Color temperature and type weight establish trust more than any copy. Warm palette with precise typography signals competence and approachability.
items.solution: Designed visual identity around warm off-white/charcoal with terracotta accent. Built single design-tokens.css bridging into Tailwind v4.
items.outcome: Cohesive marketing site with unified design token system, custom 3D hero animation, and instant dark mode switching.
highlights: Designed complete visual identity avoiding generic SaaS aesthetics
Built single-file design token system bridged into Tailwind v4
Created custom 3D atom canvas animation  no WebGL, no libraries
Dark mode via CSS custom properties  components never branch on theme

items.title: S.A.F.E.T.Y.™ Platform
items.subtitle: Product Design & Full-Stack Engineering
items.description: Designed and built a self-service platform that replaced a manual, staff-dependent operation with an end-to-end digital product  handling psychometric assessments, real-time scoring, instant PDF reports, team surveys, payments, and AI coaching.
items.problem: ABL's flagship psychometric products were delivered entirely by hand  coaches distributed access codes via email, results lived in a staff-only backend, and every new client meant more manual work. The team couldn't scale without scaling headcount.
items.insight: The bottleneck wasn't the assessment content  it was the delivery model. Users needed autonomy. The business needed zero-touch operations. The design challenge was making a complex psychometric instrument feel approachable enough for self-service.
items.solution: Designed an information architecture that separated three user journeys (individual assessment, team survey management, coaching) into clear navigation paths. Built a progressive disclosure pattern for the 50-question assessment that maintained focus without overwhelming. Created an instant browser-side PDF report system that eliminated server queues entirely.
items.outcome: Assessment completions increased 483% vs. prior year. Zero staff intervention required for the complete user journey. The platform handles onboarding, scoring, reports, payments, and CRM sync autonomously.
highlights: Designed the end-to-end user journey for a complex psychometric assessment
Assessment completions increased 483% vs. prior year average
Created progressive disclosure pattern for 50-question assessments
Designed instant client-side report generation  zero wait time
Built scoring visualizations that make percentile data understandable
items.challenge:
ABL ran their two flagship products manually. There was no user-facing platform  coaches distributed access by hand, assessment results lived in a staff admin backend, and every new client meant more work for the same small team.

The brief was to build the product that didn't exist: a self-service platform where users could sign up, take the assessment, receive their report, and manage team surveys without anyone at ABL needing to get involved.

sections.title: Design Direction
sections.body:
ABL was specific about how the platform should look and feel. They didn't want a generic internal tool  they wanted something that reflected the quality of the work they do, and that their users could actually navigate without friction.

The UI leans dark with deliberate use of color: accents that direct attention without demanding it, plenty of breathing room around data, and typography that keeps scores and analytics readable at a glance regardless of how much is on screen.

Because the platform deals with sensitive psychometric data, clarity mattered more than cleverness. Every layout decision was about making the right information visible at the right time  nothing more.

sections.title: The Assessment
sections.body:
The S.A.F.E.T.Y. model is a neuroscience-based framework that measures how strongly a person responds to six psychological domains: Security, Autonomy, Fairness, Esteem, Trust, and You. Users complete 50 questions in 1015 minutes, and the platform returns scores that reflect not just their answers  but where they stand relative to everyone who has taken the assessment before them.

The platform delivers this in two tiers. The free assessment gives users a basic view of their domain scores. The professional tier  unlocked with a single-use access code  produces a full multi-page report with detailed domain impact analysis and a personalized narrative.

Scoring runs in real time against the full population of prior responses. Every submission is ranked, and the system is built to hold that speed well past 100,000 assessments without changes.

sections.title: PS Pulse
sections.body:
PS Pulse is the team-level complement to the individual assessment. Team leaders create named teams, generate per-team access codes, distribute a survey link, and receive aggregated domain scores on a dedicated dashboard. Survey cycles can be repeated  the platform tracks trends over time so leaders can see whether things are improving, stalling, or shifting.

Reports can be downloaded, emailed, or compared side-by-side against previous cycles directly in the dashboard.

sections.title: Reports
sections.body: Every professional S.A.F.E.T.Y. assessment produces a full multi-page PDF report generated entirely in the browser, instantly, with no server involved. A user submits their assessment and the report is ready to download in the same moment.

sections.title: Payment & Integrations
sections.body: Payments happen through ABL's existing CRM. When a purchase completes, a webhook fires to the platform  which identifies the buyer, generates access codes, and emails confirmation. No one at ABL touches anything.
